data.title: Hungary's retail sales rise 5.0% in annual terms in April- stats
data.description:
Hungary's calendar-adjusted retail sales (HURETY=ECI) rose by an annual 5.0% in April following a revised 0.8% rise in March, the Central Statistics Office (KSH) said on Friday.

Food sales rose by 3.7% year-on-year, while non-food sales rose by 4.0%. Fuel sales increase by 2.4% in annual terms, the KSH said.

data.title: Hungary's April output fell by 5.0% y/y, more than expected -stats
data.description:
Hungary's industrial output (HUIND=ECI) fell by an annual 5.0% in April based on preliminary unadjusted data, more than analysts' forecast of a 4.5% drop, the Central Statistics Office (KSH) said on Friday.

In March industrial output remained unchanged compared to the previous year.

Adjusted for the effect of working days, output fell by 2.3% year-on-year, while in monthly terms, it surged by 1.5%, the KSH said.

The KSH said production volume decreased in most of the manufacturing subsections compared to the same month of the previous year. The largest decrease was observed in the manufacture of transport equipment, in the manufacture of electrical equipment, as well as in the manufacture of food products, beverages and tobacco products, while the manufacture of computer, electronic and optical products grew.

In the first four months of 2025, industrial output fell by 4.5% in annual terms.

data.title: Polish foreign minister takes aim at Musk after Trump clash
data.description:
Poland's foreign minister poked fun at Elon Musk late on Thursday, returning to a social media spat from March after the Tesla and SpaceX boss spectacularly fell out with U.S. President Donald Trump.

Warsaw's top diplomat Radoslaw Sikorski found himself embroiled in an extraordinarily public clash with Musk and U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio in March after he said Ukraine may need an alternative to the Starlink satellite service.

Amid a flurry of posts on his social media platform X, Musk had told Sikorski to "Be quiet, small man".

On Thursday simmering tensions between Musk and Trump exploded into a public feud, as the president threatened to cut off government contracts to companies run by the world's richest man. Musk suggested Trump should be impeached.

Sikorski took aim at Musk in a post on X, saying "See, big man, politics is harder than you thought."

There was no immediate response to the post from Musk.

data.title: HSBC Buys Back Shares
data.description:
HSBC repurchased 2,388,143 shares on Thursday, according to a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing.

The lender repurchased 1,516,943 shares in the UK at a volume-weighted average price of 8.7454 pounds sterling apiece and 871,200 shares in Hong Kong for HK$93.0436 per share.

HSBC has so far repurchased 134,579,894 shares for a little over $1.57 billion since the current buyback program began May 6.

Following the cancellation of shares repurchased in the UK, and after cancellation of certain other repurchased shares, the firm has 17,531,794,643 ordinary shares in issue.

data.title: NTPC Green Begins Commercial Operations of Khavda Solar Power Project
data.description: NTPC Renewable Energy, a subsidiary of NTPC Green Energy , has declared the first part capacity of 110.25 MW out of 1,255 MW Khavda-I solar PV project as commercially operational, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

data.title: Zhifei Biological Products' Pneumonia Vaccine Enters Production Registration Review
data.description:
Chongqing Zhifei Biological Products said its PCV15 vaccine has entered production registration review by China's drug regulator.

The vaccine targets 15 pneumococcal strains prevalent in Asia, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The pharmaceutical company's shares were down 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Nomura Sees More Rate Cuts in India as Growth Slows, Inflation Cools
data.description:
India's economy is showing signs of a Goldilocks phase with strong Q1 GDP growth and easing inflation, but underlying weakness may prompt further rate cuts, Nomura said in a report.

The brokerage raised its FY26 GDP growth forecast to 6.2% from 5.8% after Q1 growth beat expectations at 7.4% year-on-year, driven by government capex and net exports.

However, weak private consumption, sluggish private investment and global headwinds persist.

Nomura lowered its FY26 inflation forecast from 3.9% to 3.3%, citing soft food prices, lower input costs, and a favourable monsoon outlook.

With inflation well below the RBI's 4% target and growth still below trend, Nomura expects four 25-basis-point rate cuts, bringing the policy rate to 5% by year-end.

It sees liquidity remaining in surplus to support transmission and expects the government to meet its 4.4% fiscal deficit target, helped by RBI dividends and higher fuel taxes.

data.title: Danish Manufacturing Output Recovers in April
data.description:
Denmark’s manufacturing production rose by 2% month-over-month in April 2025, rebounding from a 3.2% drop in the previous month.

The recovery was largely driven by a strong rebound in the output of machinery (25.7% vs -13.9% in March), electrical equipment (11.7% vs -3.7%), and transport equipment (4.2% vs -6.7%).

Additionally, production continued to increase for electronic components (8.3% vs 1.5%) and wood, paper products and printing (0.8% vs 0.2%).

Meanwhile, activity declined for basic metals and fabricated metal products (-3.4% vs 2.9%) and pharmaceuticals (-5.3% vs -3.8%).

On a non-seasonally adjusted yearly basis, manufacturing output decreased by 15.4% in April, following a 2.5% fall in the preceding period.

data.title: Trainline Chosen by Rail Delivery Group to Supply Digital Payment Technology
data.description:
By Andrea Figueras

Trainline said it has been selected by Rail Delivery Group as a technology supplier to support one of four digital pay-as-you-go trials.

The U.K. rail and bus ticket website said Friday that it has been selected as a preferred bidder, but a formal contract hasn't been signed yet.

The trials will run across the Northern Rail and East Midlands Railway networks. They will start between September and November, with each running for nine months from their respective start date.

This digital payment option could potentially be rolled out to cities and regions across the U.K., the company said.

Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com

data.title: Nomura Keeps Malaysia's 2025 GDP Growth Forecast Steady, Cuts Inflation Forecast
data.description:
Nomura kept its 2025 GDP growth forecast for Malaysia at 4.4%, citing resilient domestic demand and robust investment momentum, according to its latest Asia Economic Monthly report published Friday.

However, it lowered its 2025 headline inflation forecast for Malaysia to 1.8% from 2.4%, although it expects price pressures to rise in the second half due to the upcoming Ron95 fuel subsidy rationalization.

Meanwhile, the Japanese research firm expects Bank Negara Malaysia to hold its overnight policy rate steady at 3.0% this year.

On the fiscal side, Nomura sees the deficit narrowing to 3.8% of GDP in 2025 from 4.1% in 2024, in line with the government's target.

data.title: K.S. Terminals' Revenue Drops 9% in May
data.description:
K.S. Terminals' consolidated revenue dropped 8.9% to NT$349 million in May from NT$383 million a year prior.

For January to May, the revenue slid by 2.24% to NT$1.75 billion from NT$1.79 billion a year prior.

data.title: German Exports Drop More than Expected
data.description:
Exports from Germany fell by 1.7% month-over-month to a three-month low of EUR 131.1 billion in April 2025, reversing an upwardly revised 1.2% gain in the previous month and coming in worse than market forecasts of a 0.5% decline.

The drop was mainly due to lower sales to the US, China, and the UK. Exports to non-EU countries outside these major markets contracted by 4.8%, driven primarily by a 10.5% plunge in shipments to the U.S., dampened by tariffs imposed under the Trump administration.

Shipments to China and the UK also declined, by 5.9% and 2.1%, respectively.

By contrast, exports to the European Union rose by 0.9%, with shipments to the Euro area up 0.5% and those to non-Euro area EU countries increasing by 1.8%.

data.title: Citi downgrades Airbus to 'neutral' on impact of weak USD
data.description:
** Citi downgrades European aircraft manufacturer Airbus to "neutral" from "buy" citing an impact from weak U.S. dollar

** The brokerage says strong euro against U.S. dollar raises Airbus' costs, affecting its valuation despite the company's near-term hedging of currency exposure

** Citi cuts its PT for Airbus by 12% to 183 euros

** "We estimate 1 cent USD weakening reduces Airbus fair value by EUR 3-3.5 per share," it says

** The EUR/USD exchange rate is a principal driver of value for Airbus alongside long-term production rates of its A320 aircraft, the broker notes

** It expects Airbus' mid-term growth to be lower than its peers, and says lower valuation multiples are appropriate

** Out of 20 analysts that cover Airbus, 16 rate it "strong buy" or "buy", ​three "hold" and one "sell" - LSEG data

data.title: Exclusive: Aon crisis management director for credit and political risk Minor exits
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Aon's director of crisis management for structured credit and political risk John Minor has exited the broker, two senior sources told The Insurer.

The sources said that Minor had left to take up another position within the industry. The Insurer could not confirm this role prior to publication.

Chicago-based Minor has spent nearly a decade at Aon. He joined in November 2015 from his role as executive vice president and managing director at Alliant Emerging Markets.

Prior to this, he spent a further 11 years at Aon, latterly as director of political risk.

Aon declined to comment.
